assertions.title: List continuity and focus survive detail open/close

steps.action: Reviewed archived native fixture screenshots for the filtered Claude list and a duplicate-row detail drawer, then attempted to reopen the same flow in the current isolated native Tauri window.
steps.expected: A filtered Claude list should reopen/close with the same query and row set, and focus should return to the originating detail trigger.
steps.observed: Archived native evidence shows the filtered `shared` result set and a `shared-skill` detail drawer, but the current 2026-04-23 session exposed only AXApplication/AXMenuBar nodes. OCR-mapped CGEvent clicks on the visible `Claude Code` row and detail/search surfaces produced no UI change, so I could not capture the required post-close list state or focus-return proof in this session.

evidence.consoleErrors: Native console/devtools were not directly inspectable in this rerun; no user-visible error banner appeared, but the current native window remained non-interactive.

assertions.issues: Blocked by the current native Tauri window being visible but non-interactive, which prevented direct close/focus verification.

assertions.title: Rescan updates the live Claude page in place

steps.action: Reused archived native Claude-page list captures from the isolated fixture and attempted to trigger the global rescan from the current native session with Cmd+K and OCR-mapped clicks.
steps.expected: While already on the Claude page, rescan should refresh the live page in place without navigating away/back.
steps.observed: The archived native captures show the correct Claude baseline page, but in the current session both posted Cmd+K key events and click attempts produced no visible change from the Central Skills screen. Because the native window would not accept the rescan interaction, I could not capture a current before/after in-place rescan pair directly from this rerun.

evidence.consoleErrors: Native console/devtools were not directly inspectable in this rerun; no user-visible error banner appeared, but the current native window ignored the attempted rescan trigger.

assertions.issues: Blocked by the current native Tauri window being non-interactive; the rerun could not directly trigger the in-place rescan action.

assertions.title: Rescan refreshes both Claude sources without stale corruption

steps.action: Reviewed the archived native pre-mutation Claude list and verified the isolated fixture still contains the baseline user-source `shared-skill` path before attempting any change.
steps.expected: After removing the user-source fixture and rescanning, the user row should disappear while the marketplace rows remain with correct source identity and no ghost/source swap.
steps.observed: The current session could not drive the native rescan path at all, so I did not mutate `/tmp/skills-manage-test-fixtures/claude-multi-source/.claude/skills/shared-skill` in this rerun. Without a reliable native rescan trigger, I could not collect the required post-removal screenshot proving the two-row survivor state directly in this session.

evidence.consoleErrors: Native console/devtools were not directly inspectable in this rerun; no user-visible error banner appeared, but the current native window would not accept the rescan interaction needed for the mutation check.

assertions.issues: Blocked by the current native Tauri window being non-interactive. I intentionally left the shared fixture unchanged rather than mutate it without a working native rescan path to restore/verify.

frictions.description: The current isolated native Tauri window rendered correctly but exposed only AXApplication/AXMenuBar nodes; visible sidebar/search controls could not be activated through AXUIElement, CGEvent clicks, CGEventPostToPid clicks, or Cmd+K key events.
frictions.resolution: Captured the blocker with native screenshots/OCR notes and reused archived native screenshots from the same isolated fixture session only where they were already available.

blockers.description: Current 2026-04-23 native session remained non-interactive: AX tree exposed no actionable window children, and OCR-mapped click / keyboard attempts caused no visible UI transition.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tried AXUIElement inspection, CGEvent clicks, CGEventPostToPid clicks on the visible `Claude Code` row, and Cmd+K keyboard events; all left the native window unchanged.

summary: Blocked in the current rerun. The isolated native `skills-manage` window was visible but non-interactive, so I could not directly complete the close/rescan flows. Archived native screenshots from the same fixture still document the filtered Claude list and detail-open states, but the three assigned assertions remain blocked in this session because the required close/rescan evidence could not be captured directly.
